ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* Patient's first name */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Patient's last name */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Patient's date of birth */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Patient's contact email */,
  "address" TEXT /* Patient's address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Patient's phone number */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Emergency contact name */,
  "emergency_contact_phone_number" TEXT /* Emergency contact phone number */,
  "insurance_provider" TEXT /* Patient's insurance provider */,
  "insurance_number" TEXT /* Patient's insurance number */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare provider */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* Healthcare provider's first name */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Healthcare provider's last name */,
  "specialty" TEXT /* Healthcare provider's specialty */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Healthcare provider's contact email */,
  "practice_name" TEXT /* Name of healthcare provider's practice */,
  "practice_address" TEXT /* Address of healthcare provider's practice */,
  "practice_phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of healthcare provider's practice */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medications" (
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication */,
  "medication_name" TEXT /* Medication name */,
  "dosage_form" TEXT /* Dosage form (e.g., tablet, capsule) */,
  "strength" TEXT /* Medication strength */,
  "route_of_administration" TEXT /* Route of administration (e.g., oral, topical) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of administration */,
  "indication" TEXT /* Indication for medication use */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prescriptions" (
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prescription */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient the prescription belongs to */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the healthcare provider who prescribed the medication */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication prescribed */,
  "dosage_instructions" TEXT /* Dosage instructions for the medication */,
  "quantity" TEXT /* Quantity of medication prescribed */,
  "refills" INTEGER /* Number of refills allowed */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the prescription expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prescription_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES healthcare_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "repeat_prescriptions" (
  "repeat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each repeat prescription */,
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the prescription the repeat belongs to */,
  "number_of_repeats" INTEGER /* Number of repeats authorized */,
  "supporting_information" TEXT /* Supporting information for the repeat prescription */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the repeat prescription (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("repeat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_repeat_prescriptions_prescription_id FOREIGN KEY ("prescription_id") REFERENCES prescriptions ("prescription_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dispensing" (
  "dispensing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dispensing event */,
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the prescription being dispensed */,
  "date_dispensed" TEXT /* Date the medication was dispensed */,
  "quantity_dispensed" TEXT /* Quantity of medication dispensed */,
  "dispensing_pharmacy" TEXT /* Name of the dispensing pharmacy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dispensing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dispensing_prescription_id FOREIGN KEY ("prescription_id") REFERENCES prescriptions ("prescription_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication interaction */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication */,
  "interacting_medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interacting medication */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., contraindication, warning) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the interaction (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_interactions_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_interactions_interacting_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("interacting_medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_medication_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient medication history record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication */,
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the prescription */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the medication regimen */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the medication regimen */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the medication regimen (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_medication_history_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_medication_history_prescription_id FOREIGN KEY ("prescription_id") REFERENCES prescriptions ("prescription_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_medication_history_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_administration" (
  "administration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication administration record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication */,
  "dose_given" TEXT /* Dose of medication given */,
  "administration_date" TEXT /* Date the medication was administered */,
  "administration_time" TEXT /* Time the medication was administered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("administration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_administration_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_administration_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "adverse_reactions" (
  "reaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each adverse reaction record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication */,
  "reaction_date" TEXT /* Date the adverse reaction occurred */,
  "reaction_description" TEXT /* Description of the adverse reaction */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the adverse reaction (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adverse_reactions_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adverse_reactions_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., healthcare provider, pharmacist, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_allergies" (
  "allergy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient allergy record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "allergen" TEXT /* Allergen (e.g., medication, food, environmental) */,
  "reaction" TEXT /* Reaction to the allergen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allergy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_allergies_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication order */,
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the prescription the order belongs to */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication ordered */,
  "quantity_ordered" TEXT /* Quantity of medication ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_orders_prescription_id FOREIGN KEY ("prescription_id") REFERENCES prescriptions ("prescription_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_orders_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication inventory record */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication */,
  "quantity_in_stock" TEXT /* Quantity of medication in stock */,
  "reorder_point" TEXT /* Reorder point (e.g., when the quantity falls below this level, reorder) */,
  "reorder_quantity" TEXT /* Reorder quantity (e.g., the amount to reorder when the quantity falls below the reorder point) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_inventory_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)
